name: Miniature Pumpkin Soup in the Shell
recipeIngredient: 8 mini pumpkins (each about the size of a large tomato)
1/2 tbsp minced fresh sage or 1 teaspoon dried sage
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the top quarter off 4 pumpkins, reserving the lids and seeds.
2. Discard the seeds from the remaining pumpkins.
3. Halve the remaining pumpkins and cut them with the cut side down on lightly oiled baking sheets in a preheated 350°F oven for 40 minutes or until tender (remove lids after about 20 minutes).
4. Scrape all the pulp out of the halved pumpkins.
5. In a skillet, cook the onion in butter over low heat, stirring, until softened. Add sage and cook for 2 minutes.
6. Add pumpkin pulp and chicken stock to the skillet; simmer for 20 minutes.
7. Puree mixture in batches in a blender; transfer to saucepan.
8. Stir soup with salt and pepper to taste; add water if needed to thin consistency.
9. Ladle about 1/2 cup of soup into each of 4 heated bowls; place one hollowed-out pumpkin shell on top. Fill the shell with soup, garnish with Parmesan and parsley, then cover with reserved lids.
Yield: 4 servings
